markdownRemark.excerpt: Hi, I’m Chris Bautista — a frontend engineer who specializes in accessible, production-grade web interfaces.
markdownRemark.html:
Hi, I’m Chris Bautista — a frontend engineer who specializes in accessible, production-grade web interfaces.

Most of my recent work sits at the intersection of accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) and modern frontend engineering: auditing components for screen reader and keyboard support, fixing real-world issues like focus traps, missing alt text, and unlabeled controls, and writing about what I learn along the way so other developers can skip the trial and error.

#What I work with

  • Languages: JavaScript, TypeScript
  • Frontend: React, Gatsby, Tailwind CSS
  • Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA audits, ARIA patterns, keyboard/screen-reader testing, semantic HTML
  • Testing: Vitest
  • Other: Node.js, REST APIs, CSS (container queries, modern layout)

#Recent case studies

  • Build an Accessible Tab Container — roving tabindex, arrow-key navigation, ARIA tab pattern
  • Fix accessibility using focus-within — solving keyboard-vs-mouse UI mismatches without JavaScript
  • Automatically generate skip buttons — scripted skip-navigation links
  • Accessible UI Component Series — an accordion, buttons, and shared design tokens built to test ARIA patterns firsthand

Also see Creating My Animated Bottom Bar Experiment, my most-loved CodePen experiment — interaction and motion rather than accessibility, but a good look at range.

See the full portfolio or browse posts tagged accessibility for more.

#A bit of background

I’ve been building for the web for over 15 years — from hybrid mobile apps (Cordova, jQuery Mobile) and PHP/REST backends to, more recently, React and TypeScript. These days accessibility is the thread that ties most of my work together.

I still own CodeSpud Web Services, and you can find my code on GitHub. Have a challenge I can help with? Post it as a comment or email me at challenges at codespud dot com.

Outside of code: gaming, cooking, and chasing the perfect cup of coffee.
